status: DRAFT — pending directors' resolution; not yet anchored. Rev 2: M4MINT attributed 2026-06-12.
raw_check_policy: chain_integrity_shift_events is NEVER filtered — it stays RED while any listed event exists. chain_integrity_shift_events_ex_baseline is the operational signal.

PILOT_SPEC_FLUX.explanation: spec_version 0 events written 2026-04-30..2026-05-11 while the WLES hash specification was in flux pre-v1.0-cutover (cutover 2026-06-04). Mismatch is a documented historical artefact of the spec change, not tampering. CHAIN_BREAK alerts for these events have fired daily since 2026-04-28 and remain on the immutable record (504 alert rows at adoption time).

SYNTHETIC_TEST_FIXTURE.explanation: Deliberate prod-side planted-break fixture from the 2026-06-06 bulletproof-harness session (PR #44, merged 2026-06-06T22:53Z). Attribution evidence: (1) PR #44 body describes the exercise verbatim — a v1 row with a non-matching previous_event_hash planted 'via the direct service-role write path; the INSERT succeeds... the planted break is DETECTED'; (2) that session's milestone vocabulary names M4 as the export-packs/type-registry milestone ('until the M4 type-registry-lock work merges', tests/integration-postgres/bulletproof.test.ts) and uses the all-9s synthetic-id convention; (3) the fixture family is self-labelled — workers row 99999999-...-991 is named 'M4MINT Synthetic Test Worker', created 2026-06-06T04:52:36Z, 57s before the event, in the standing test company; (4) founder reviewed 2026-06-12 and did not recognise it as external. The prod plant was never documented at the time — that process miss is the only defect; the daily detection of this event since 2026-06-06 is the sweep working as designed.

adoption.anchoring: After adoption, register the SHA-256 of this file as a substrate_anchors row so the exceptions list is covered by the anchor_fingerprint check and cannot be quietly amended.
